title: Customer support chat: composer gated until active (dbg142)
description:
dbg141 fixed admin chat-response SSE filtering and added /chat/active polling, but Mike and Kat could still see mismatched UI: admin "Waiting for accept" with a live composer and messages, customer "Support request sent" alongside chat messages.

Root cause (dbg142): showChatMode(true) without { live: true } re-exposed the status body on every chat-message SSE event, restoring stale hints ("Waiting for accept", "Support request sent") while the composer stayed enabled. Admin adminStartChat also opened the composer before the customer accepted even though POST /chat/messages rejects pending sessions.

Fix: live chat mode now exclusively controls the composer (disabled until session status is active). Admin pending shows status only — no composer. chat-message events use live mode and auto-promote admin pending→active via loadChatSessionIntoWidget. Customer prep/accept clears stale support-request status. setStatus no longer breaks live mode.

Includes uncommitted dbg140–141 backend (chat/active, chat-response adminUserId, terminate all sessions on chat/start) and frontend (supportUserId filter, poll sync).

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare customer support chat on the OVH private server. Deploy both frontend and backend together.
changeLog: Widget: live mode gates composer; setStatus cannot unhide body during live chat.
Admin: pending invite shows status only — composer hidden until accept or poll/SSE activates.
chat-message SSE uses live mode; admin auto-activates UI when message arrives on pending session.
Customer: clear stale "Support request sent" before invite modal and on accept.
onSendMessage blocked until session is active (both sides).
Backend (dbg140–141): GET /chat/active, chat-response adminUserId, terminate all sessions on chat/start.
Cache bust dbg142 (portal runtime + PipeSync manifest).
